title: 10 best herbal teas for itchy eyes

remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: The tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as apigenin and luteolin, help to reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes caused by inflammation.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in Purple Coneflower tea, including quercetin and kaempferol, neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation and discomfort, soothing itchy eyes.

Antihistamine activity: The tea's antihistamine properties, primarily attributed to the presence of apigenin, help to alleviate itchy eyes caused by allergies or histamine reactions, providing relief from itching, burning, and redness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of purple coneflower contain anti-inflammatory compounds that can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Barks: The barks of the plant are rich in flavonoids, which have been shown to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness associated with eye irritations.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh echinacea purpurea leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested material thoroughly with clean water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine 1 teaspoon of dried or 2 teaspoons of fresh echinacea purpurea with 8 ounces of boiling water in a tea infuser or heat-resistant cup.
Allow the mixture to steep for 5-7 minutes, then strain and discard the solids using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Enjoy the cooled herbal tea immediately, drinking 2-3 cups per day as needed for itchy eye relief, taking 30 minutes before bedtime.
remedies_list.remedy_desc: Purple coneflower teas helps with itchy eyes because of its potent anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated conjunctiva. The tea's flavonoids and terpenes work to reduce inflammation and relieve redness, while its astringent qualities help to shrink swollen blood vessels and ease itchiness. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties combat infections that can cause eye irritation, providing long-lasting relief from itchy eyes.

Pot marigold teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tea's antioxidant-rich composition also helps to reduce redness and swelling associated with itchiness. Additionally, pot marigold contains flavonoids, which have been shown to have a natural antihistamine effect, providing relief from itching and discomfort caused by allergies or irritants. As the tea is absorbed through the eyes, it provides fast and effective relief for itchy eyes, making it an excellent natural remedy for promoting eye comfort and health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Pot marigold tea's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as flavonoids and saponins, help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eyes.
Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in pot marigold tea, including vitamin A and beta-carotene, neutralize free radicals that can damage the eyes and exacerbate itchiness and discomfort.

Calming and soothing properties: Pot marigold tea's calming compounds, such as sesquiterpenes and terpenoids, have a natural sedative effect on the eyes, helping to reduce itchiness, irritation, and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Contain anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Bark: Rich in flavonoids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties that alleviate itchiness and redness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest one teaspoon of dried pot marigold flowers calendula officinalis from a trusted source.
Rinse the dried flowers with 200ml boiling water to rehydrate for five minutes.
Steep the rehydrated flowers in 200ml boiling water for seven to nine minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh strainer or cheesecloth and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool down to room temperature before using as an eye wash within thirty minutes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Aloe teas helps with itchy eyes because its soothing properties calm inflammation and reduce redness. The antioxidants present in aloe vera, such as vitamins A, C, and E, work to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate eye irritation. Additionally, the tea's anti-inflammatory compounds help to ease itchiness and discomfort associated with conditions like dry eyes, allergies, and conjunctivitis. As a result, drinking herbal aloe teas can provide quick relief from itchy, irritated eyes, promoting overall eye health and comfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Aloe vera's anti-inflammatory properties reduce redness, swelling, and irritation associated with itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort.
Antimicrobial: The antimicrobial properties in aloe tea help combat infections that can cause itchiness and inflammation in the eyes, promoting a healthy environment for vision.
Soothing and moisturizing: Aloe vera's soothing and moisturizing effects calm and hydrate the eyes, reducing itchiness and dryness caused by conditions like conjunctivitis or allergies.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: Contain soothing compounds that reduce redness and inflammation, providing instant relief from itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh aloe vera leaves from your garden or purchase them at a health store measuring about 10-12 inches long.
Rinse the aloe vera leaves under cold running water to remove any dirt or debris gently scrubbing the skin with a soft brush if needed.
Cut off the edges of the leaves and chop them into small pieces to release the juice allowing it to flow freely.
Combine 1-2 tablespoons of chopped aloe vera with 1 cup of boiling filtered water steeping for 5-7 minutes then straining the liquid carefully.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before using as an eye wash or compress applying a few drops directly to your closed eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Goldenseal teas helps with itchy eyes because of its natural anti-inflammatory properties. The active compound berberine in goldenseal soothes and calms irritated eye tissues, reducing redness and itchiness caused by allergies or environmental irritants. Additionally, the tea's antimicrobial properties help combat infections that may be contributing to the discomfort. By promoting healing and reducing inflammation, goldenseal teas provide quick relief from itchy eyes, making it a natural remedy for people seeking alternative treatments.
remedy_properties: Berberine: Has antibacterial, antiviral, and anti-inflammatory effects, which helps to reduce inflammation and combat infections in the eyes, alleviating itchiness and redness.

Berberastain: Possesses antimicrobial properties that can help to eliminate bacterial overgrowth in the eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort associated with conjunctivitis and other eye infections.
Isoquinoline alkaloids: Exert a relaxing effect on the eyes, helping to reduce inflammation and alleviate itchiness caused by allergies or irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots contain berberine, a bioactive compound that helps to reduce inflammation and alleviate itching associated with itchy eyes.
Rhyzomes: The rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe red and irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried goldenseal root powder containing at least 4% berberine alkaloids for optimal benefits.
Measure one teaspoon of the powdered goldenseal root and combine it with one cup of boiling water in a teapot or infuser.
Steep the mixture for exactly 10 minutes to allow the active compounds to release and mix with the water.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove any sediment or residue.
Drink the warm goldenseal tea immediately after straining, holding it in your mouth for one minute before swallowing to allow the berberine to take effect.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Comfrey teas helps with itchy eyes because its natural anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties soothe and calm irritated conjunctiva, reducing redness and discomfort. The herb's ability to promote healing and reduce inflammation also helps to alleviate itchiness caused by allergies or dryness. Additionally, comfrey's moisturizing qualities help to hydrate the eyes, leaving them feeling refreshed and soothed. By drinking comfrey tea regularly, individuals can enjoy long-lasting relief from itchy eyes without relying on harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory: Comfrey tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness, swelling, and irritation in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.
Antioxidant: The antioxidants present in comfrey tea protect the delicate tissues of the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which can exacerbate itchy eye symptoms and promote healing.

Soothing and anti-irritant: Comfrey's mucilages (gum-like compounds) create a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, soothing irritated tissues, reducing itchiness, and calming dryness or irritation.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in allantoin, a natural anti-inflammatory compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Buds: Containing high levels of rosmarinic acid, a powerful antioxidant that reduces inflammation and promotes healing.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh comfrey leaves in the morning after dew has dried off and rinse them gently with cold water.
Chop 10-15 grams of comfrey leaves into small pieces and place them in a clean glass jar or container.
Pour one cup of boiling water over the chopped comfrey leaves and let it steep for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the liquid through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a clean teapot or cup and discard solids.
Allow the tea to cool slightly then use as an eye wash by dipping a clean cloth into the tea and gently applying it to affected eyes.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Eyebright teas helps with itchy eyes because of its unique combination of herbs that work together to soothe and calm the affected area. The tea's anti-inflammatory properties help to reduce redness and swelling, while its antihistamine qualities alleviate itching and irritation. Additionally, eyebright tea's decongestant properties help to relieve excess tearing and discharge, further reducing itchiness and discomfort. As a result, eyebright teas provide effective relief from itchy eyes, promoting healthy and comfortable vision.
remedy_properties: Antihistamine activity: Eyebright tea's antihistamine properties help to reduce itching and inflammation caused by allergies, allergic reactions, or other irritants.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in eyebright tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness and discomfort.

Antioxidant activity: Eyebright tea's antioxidant properties help to neutralize free radicals that can contribute to oxidative stress, which can exacerbate itchy eye symptoms and slow down the healing process.
remedy_parts: Roots: Used for its anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, eyebright roots help soothe and calm itchy eyes.
Seeds: Rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, eyebright seeds provide relief from itchiness and dryness associated with irritated eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh euphrasia officinalis leaves or purchase dried herb from a reputable source measuring out one teaspoon per cup of boiling water.
Boil clean filtered water in a tea kettle or pot bringing it to a rolling boil for exactly three minutes before using.
Combine the harvested or dried euphrasia officinalis with the boiling water allowing it to steep for five to seven minutes releasing its natural oils and compounds.
Strain the tea mixture through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth into a cup or teapot discarding the solids and retaining only the liquid infusion.
Allow the cooled tea to sit at room temperature for thirty minutes before refrigerating it and using it as needed up to two days later.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dandelion teas helps with itchy eyes because its anti-inflammatory properties soothe and calm irritated eyes. The tea's potent antioxidants, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, work to reduce redness and swelling, while also alleviating dryness and itchiness caused by allergies or environmental factors. Additionally, dandelion tea's natural astringent properties help to constrict blood vessels, reducing the release of histamine that can exacerbate itchy eyes, providing relief from discomfort and promoting overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant Properties: Dandelion tea's antioxidants can help reduce inflammation and oxidative stress in the eyes, which may contribute to itchiness and irritation.

Anti-Inflammatory Compounds: The presence of anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids in dandelion tea may help soothe and calm itchy, irritated eyes by reducing swelling and redness.

Vasodilatory Effects: Dandelion tea's vasodilatory properties can help improve blood flow to the eyes, which may reduce itchiness and irritation by allowing for better oxygenation and nourishment of the ocular tissues.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, dandelion roots can help soothe and calm irritated eyes.
Leaves: Containing bioflavonoids and vitamins, dandelion leaves may help reduce inflammation and ease itchiness associated with eye issues.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh dandelion leaves and flowers in the early morning or late afternoon when dewy for optimal potency.
Rinse the harvested plant material gently with cold running water to remove any dirt or debris.
Combine one teaspoon of dried dandelion root and two teaspoons of dried dandelion flowers with one cup of boiling water.
Steep the mixture for 10-15 minutes, allowing the herbs to infuse their medicinal properties into the tea.
Strain the tea through a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth and discard solids before serving warm or cooled as needed.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Marshmallow teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties, which soothe and calm irritated eye tissues. The mucilage in marshmallows forms a protective barrier on the surface of the eyes, reducing redness and itching caused by dry air, allergies, or exposure to irritants. This natural remedy also moisturizes the eyes, helping to rebalance the delicate tear film and promote healthy blinking habits, ultimately providing relief from itchy, irritated eyes.
remedy_properties: Mucilages: Marshmallow tea contains mucilages, which are thick, protective substances that can soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing itchiness by providing a barrier against irritants.

Anti-inflammatory compounds: Marshmallow tea is rich in anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which may help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes by inhibiting the production of pro-inflammatory chemicals.

Antioxidants: The antioxidants present in marshmallow tea can help neutralize free radicals that can contribute to eye irritation and itchiness, promoting overall eye health and reducing oxidative stress.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the marshmallow plant contain mucilage, a soothing substance that helps to calm irritated skin and relieve itchiness.
Seeds: Marshmallow seeds are rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and swelling associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried althaea officinalis root or leaves in amounts of one teaspoon per cup.
Combine the dried herbs with boiling water in a tea infuser or a heat-resistant cup.
Steep the mixture for 5-7 minutes allowing the herbal properties to release and mix with the liquid.
Strain the tea into a clean cup and discard the solids using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth.
Let the tea cool slightly before consuming it immediately or storing it in an airtight container for up to 24 hours.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Stinging nettle teas helps with itchy eyes because of its anti-inflammatory properties. The tea's active compounds, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, work to reduce swelling and redness in the eyes, providing relief from irritation and discomfort. Additionally, stinging nettle's antihistamine effects help to alleviate itchiness caused by allergies or dry eyes, promoting a soothing and calming sensation in the eyes. As a result, drinking stinging nettle tea can provide natural and effective relief for itchy eyes.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Stinging nettle tea contains anti-inflammatory compounds like flavonoids and terpenoids, which can help reduce inflammation in the eyes and alleviate itchiness caused by conditions such as conjunctivitis or allergic reactions.

Antihistamine effects: The herbal tea's antihistamine properties can help counteract the release of histamine, a chemical that causes itching, redness, and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchy eye symptoms.

Antioxidant activity: Stinging nettle tea's high antioxidant content can help protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, which may contribute to dry eye syndrome or other age-related eye problems that can lead to itchiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds, making them effective at soothing itchy eyes and reducing redness.
Seeds: Containing essential fatty acids and antioxidants, they help calm inflammation and promote healing in itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh or dried nettle leaves and flowers from trusted sources, ensuring they are free of contaminants.
Measure out one teaspoon of dried nettle leaves for every cup of boiling water, using a precise measuring spoon.
Combine the measured nettle leaves with one cup of boiling water in a large glass teapot or infuser, allowing steeping time for 5-7 minutes.
Strain the tea into a clean glass cup, discarding the solids and adding one teaspoon of honey to enhance flavor and soothe irritated eyes.
Allow the tea to cool slightly before applying it as an eye wash, gently tilting your head back to pour the tea into the affected eye.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice teas helps with itchy eyes because its active compound, glycyrrhizin, has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe and calm irritated eyes. When brewed into a tea, licorice root extracts help reduce redness and itchiness caused by dry eye syndrome, allergies, or irritation from environmental factors. The sweet and soothing flavor of the tea provides instant relief, providing moisture and comfort to itchy eyes, allowing for improved vision and overall eye health.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory effects: Licorice root contains anti-inflammatory compounds like glycyrrhizin and glabridin, which can help reduce redness and swelling in the eyes, providing relief from itchiness.

Antioxidant properties: The antioxidants present in licorice tea, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, can protect the eyes from damage caused by free radicals, reducing oxidative stress that may contribute to itchiness.
Soothing demulcent effects: Licorice root has a soothing, protective barrier-like action on mucous membranes, which can help calm irritated eyes and provide relief from itching and discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are rich in glycyrrhizin, a compound that helps soothe and calm irritated eyes, reducing itchiness and discomfort.
Barks: The barks contain flavonoids and saponins, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce redness and itching associated with itchy eyes.
remedy_recipe: Rinse dried licorice root with cold water then measure out one teaspoon of the herb for every eight ounces of boiling water.
Heat water in a teapot or saucepan until it reaches a rolling boil then reduce heat and simmer for five minutes.
Add measured licorice root to the hot water and allow it to steep for seven to ten minutes.
Strain the tea into a cup using a fine-mesh sieve or cheesecloth to remove the loose herbs and solids.
Drink the warm tea immediately after straining to help soothe and calm irritated itchy eyes.

intro_desc: Herbal teas for itchy eyes are a natural and soothing solution to alleviate discomfort and dryness caused by irritated or allergic conjunctivitis. These teas work by reducing inflammation, calming the eyes, and moisturizing the skin around them. Examples of herbal teas that help with itchy eyes include chamomile, calendula, and peppermint tea, which can be brewed and used as a warm compress to gently soothe and calm the eyes. For people suffering from itchy eyes, these teas can significantly improve their quality of life by providing quick relief and reducing the need for harsh chemical-based treatments or artificial lubricants.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat itchy eyes with herbal teas is by brewing a soothing blend of chamomile, calendula, and peppermint. Steep one teaspoon of the mixture in boiling water for 5-7 minutes, then strain and cool the liquid before applying it as an eye compress or splash into your eyes. The anti-inflammatory properties of these herbs will help reduce itchiness and redness, promoting a sense of calm and relief.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to itchy eyes that are treated with herbal teas are various skin irritations, such as eczema and acne. Tea made from chamomile, calendula, and peppermint has anti-inflammatory properties that soothe irritated skin and reduce itching and redness. Additionally, herbal teas like burdock root and dandelion tea can help to calm and balance the digestive system, which may also contribute to itchy eyes.
